---
title: "Savings-Investment Calulator"
author: "Yuan Xie"
format: dashboard
server: shiny
---

```{r}
#| context: setup
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)

# Optional: you may use this section to write auxiliary functions

```

##  {.sidebar}

```{r}
# input widget for initial amount
numericInput(
  inputId = "P",
  label = "Initial Investment ($)",
  value = 5000
)
```

```{r}
# input widget for periodic contributions
numericInput(
  inputId = "PMT",
  label = "Annual Contribution ($)",
  value = 1000
)
```

```{r}
# input widget for number of years
sliderInput(
  inputId = "n",
  label = "Number of Years",
  min = 1,
  max = 50,
  value = 20
)
```

```{r}
# input widget for rate of return
numericInput(
  inputId = "r",
  label = "Rate of Return (e.g. 0.08)",
  value = 0.08
)
```

# Timeline

```{r}
plotlyOutput(outputId = 'plot')
```

# Table

```{r}
tableOutput(outputId = 'table')
```

```{r}
#| context: server

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Auxiliary table (note that this is a reactive object)
# (adapt code to your own analysis)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
tbl = reactive({

  P = input$P
  PMT = input$PMT
  n = input$n
  r = input$r
  
  # initialize it
  year = 0:n
  amount = numeric(length = n + 1)
  
  amount[1] = P  # year 0
  
  # calculate year by year
  for (t in 2:(n + 1)) {
    amount[t] = amount[t-1] * (1 + r) + PMT
  }
  
  tibble(
    year = year,
    amount = round(amount, 2)
  )
})


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot (timeline)
# (adapt code to make a timeline according to your analysis!!!)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
output$plot <- renderPlotly({
  p <- ggplot(data = tbl(), aes(x = year, y = amount)) +
    geom_area(fill = "#A1E3F588") +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
    labs(
      title = "Investment Growth Over Time",
      x = "Year",
      y = "Amount ($)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  ggplotly(p)
})

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Table
# (adapt code to display appropriate table!!!)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
output$table <- renderTable({
  tbl()
})
```
